meta.labeler: L-NBER: NBER recession state x 50-day trend sign of the equal-weight log-price index (trend causal via t-1, label_L3 construction); no free threshold
meta.primary_variant: announcement-lagged (causal): recession=1 from start-announcement date through end-announcement date, both endpoints inclusive
meta.secondary_variant: calendar recession months (peak month through trough month, inclusive); disclosed as non-causal robustness

announcement_dates_verified.note: all four pairs match the registration's expected values exactly

meta.disclosed_omissions: DISCLOSED OMISSION (flagged, not silent): the lodged registration's design paragraph lists 'LORO + era-blocked analyses' among the inherited conventions; this run executed the gate + walk-forward + stitched battery with gross and net-25bps but did NOT run the LORO (leave-one-recession-out) or era-blocked slices. They remain owed to the register and should be run as a follow-up pass (cf. e_french_L3_loro.py) before any paper claim cites this cell as complete.

scoring_PN1_PN4.scored_against: PRIMARY (announcement-lagged) variant; walk-forward is the headline cell, stitched is the schedule-resampling secondary; gross is the registered battery, net-25bps the lodged overlay

PN1_H_SIGN.registered: the sign of Γ̂ under L-NBER predicts the dissociation outcome direction (ordering iff Γ̂ significantly positive); weak and strong forms both reported per addendum F
PN1_H_SIGN.refutation_clause: Γ̂ significantly positive with no ordering/inversion, or Γ̂ ≈ 0 with a significant ordering, under the primary variant

PN3_fragility_discriminator.registered: positive deficit under L-NBER (no threshold) -> granularity/window reading of L3 fragility; null -> artifact reading gains, said at headline prominence

scoring_PN1_PN4.PN4: all outcomes reported; one labeler, two causality variants, family-wise accounting disclosed; no selective emphasis

secondary_calendar_sign_rule.note: robustness only; PN1-PN4 were scored against the primary variant before this ran
